lessons.title: Synchronization: Implicit and Explicit Waits
lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"In the context of test automation—especially with tools like Selenium— synchronization refers to the coordination between the automated test script and the web application it is testing. It ensures that the script interacts with web elements only when they are ready to be acted upon. Without synchronization, tests may fail due to elements not being loaded, visible, or clickable at the expected times.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Synchronization: Implicit and Explicit Waits\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"This wait time is set once and applies to all elements in the script. Syntax (Java): driver.manage().timeouts().implicitlyWait(10, TimeUnit.SECONDS);","practiceTask":"Review Synchronization: Implicit and Explicit Waits and identify 3 real-world applications where this principle is used."}

lessons.content: {"simpleExplanation":"Explicit Wait An Explicit Wait is more flexible and is used to wait for specific conditions to occur before proceeding. It works with predefined conditions provided in the ExpectedConditions class (e.g., elementToBeClickable, visibilityOf, presenceOfElementLocated). Syntax (Java): WebDriverWait wait = new WebDriverWait(driver, Duration.ofSeconds(10)); wait.until(ExpectedConditions.visibilityOfElementLocated(By.id(\"example\"))); This waits for a maximum of 10 seconds until the specified element is visible.","whyItMatters":["Provides a robust foundation for advanced topics.","Improves system design and code maintainability.","Frequently asked in technical interviews."],"example":"// Example Concept: Information Technology Services\n// Apply these principles in your projects to ensure robust functionality.","summary":"Useful for dynamic content that loads at unpredictable times.
